title: IC analyst portfolio build kit; skill taxonomy S01-S26

fields.vision: IC analyst portfolio build kit; skill taxonomy S01-S26 is a preserved project packet for portfolio build kit that converts Georgetown MPAI (Master of Professional Studies in Applied Intelligence) coursework — counterterrorism papers, cyber CTI briefs, CI risk assessments, geospatial products, major academic papers — into IC-style sanitized work samples suitable for IC analyst hiring.
fields.mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.
fields.source_utilization: Writing, rhetoric, narrative design, visual culture, and presentation. Use source library writing/visual/cinema sources to strengthen public exposition and Trilogy visual/storytelling logic. Vision: Make public pages read as curated intellectual exhibits rather than dashboards. Mechanism: Extract narrative sequencing, visual perception, close reading, rhetoric, style, cinema, and presentation mechanisms. Plan: Apply after domain-source routing so style clarifies evidence rather than masking weak evidence. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need selective close-reading before adopting style doctrine. Source pool: 64 deduplicated local title candidates; 61 catalogue support rows. Examples: Narrative and Narration: Analyzing Cinematic Storytelling; Camera Lucida. Intelligence tradecraft, counterintelligence, and analytic method. Use source library intelligence titles as method authority and intelligence-domain stressors. Vision: Present intelligence pages as disciplined analytic-method and tradecraft demonstrations. Mechanism: Tie claims to collection, covert action, deception, interrogation, bias-control, and analytic-logic mechanisms. Plan: Prioritize Capstone, SATS, CI, RAG integrity, and analyst-assurance pages for close-reading. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need method-passage extraction and claim-to-source linkage. Source pool: 60 deduplicated local title candidates; 63 catalogue support rows. Examples: Improving Intelligence Analysis; Leading Intelligence Analysis. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for authors where...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. 1. Open 00_ADMIN/Portfolio_Matrix.xlsx and fill Work Product Inventory tab. 2. For each included work product: cover sheet (02_TEMPLATES/docx) + reformatted IC product (03_WORK_SAMPLE_GUIDES) + supporting matrices (02_TEMPLATES/xlsx). 3. Export all work samples to PDF. 4. "DONE" criteria: 6-10 PDF work samples with cover sheets + 2 slide decks + updated mapping workbook + portfolio talk-track script + confirmed sanitization. Wire or preserve the implementation files: No code. Heavy reliance on Office formats (DOCX templates, XLSX workbooks) because target users are HR recruiters and managers who expect polished output.
fields.remaining_gaps: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved assumptions visible.
fields.detailed_source_vision: Portfolio build kit that converts Georgetown MPAI (Master of Professional Studies in Applied Intelligence) coursework — counterterrorism papers, cyber CTI briefs, CI risk assessments, geospatial products, major academic papers — into IC-style sanitized work samples suitable for IC analyst hiring. Philosophy: "employers hire on work products, not assertions." Package provides the full scaffold (matrices, templates, skill taxonomy, sanitization rules, interview narrative) but deliberately stops short of ingesting actual course papers — those require Pass 2. Pass 1 (this zip): portfolio inventory + mapping workbook, skill taxonomy aligned to analytic tradecraft expectations, complete template set (2-pagers, briefs, annexes), sanitization & releasability guide, interview narrative worksheets (STAR + 60-second pitch + portfolio talk-track).
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: IC analyst portfolio build kit; skill taxonomy S01-S26 is a preserved project packet for portfolio build kit that converts Georgetown MPAI (Master of Professional Studies in Applied Intelligence) coursework — counterterrorism papers, cyber CTI briefs, CI risk assessments, geospatial products, major academic papers — into IC-style sanitized work samples suitable for IC analyst hiring.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.
